title: Use Kysely SQL query builder over ORM for data access
context: The TaskFlow project requires a data access strategy for PostgreSQL. The team evaluated three approaches: SQL query builder (Kysely), ORM (Prisma), and ORM (Drizzle). The project constraint states no ORM in the MVP. A structured debate (TASK-005) was conducted to evaluate the trade-offs of each approach before making this decision.
decisionDrivers: Type safety without magic or opaque abstractions
Explicit SQL control for complex queries and migrations
No ORM constraint from project requirements
Good TypeScript ecosystem integration and maintenance

options.name: Kysely
options.description: Type-safe SQL query builder for TypeScript. Builds SQL explicitly with full TypeScript inference.
pros: Full TypeScript type inference on queries
No ORM magic or hidden queries
Explicit SQL control for performance tuning
neutral: More verbose than ORM for simple CRUD operations
cons: Higher initial boilerplate for simple queries compared to ORM
No automatic schema synchronization from model definitions

options.name: Prisma ORM
options.description: Schema-first ORM with auto-generated client, migrations, and Studio GUI.
pros: Excellent developer ergonomics and GUI tooling
neutral: Large bundle size adds to deployment weight
cons: Magic behavior obscures actual SQL for debugging
Violates the no-ORM constraint from project requirements
Complex joins require raw SQL fallback reducing type safety

options.name: Drizzle ORM
options.description: TypeScript-first ORM with SQL-like query syntax and schema-as-code.
pros: SQL-like syntax closer to raw SQL than Prisma
neutral: Newer ecosystem with less community maturity
cons: Still classified as ORM, violating the project constraint
Less battle-tested than Prisma or raw query builders at this scale

rationale: Kysely is the only option that satisfies the no-ORM project constraint while providing type safety and explicit SQL control. It enables the team to write performant, debuggable queries without sacrificing TypeScript inference. The additional verbosity is acceptable given the architectural clarity it provides.

consequences.description: Full control over SQL queries enables performance optimization and debugging
No ORM magic means developers understand exactly what SQL is executed
More boilerplate required for simple CRUD operations compared to ORM alternatives
Migration files must be written manually in SQL without auto-generation

confirmation: This decision will be confirmed when TASK-006 (database migrations) is implemented using Kysely and the resulting code is reviewed in TASK-011 integration tests.
moreInformation: See docs/database-schema.yaml for the full schema definition and src/db/connection.ts for the Kysely configuration.
